title: Morocco wins vs. Scotland: World Cup score, results, stats and highlights
fetched: raw-HTML 直抓 + 抽取(apify:website-content-crawler) + 清洗（逐字、未经任何模型/改写/翻译）
original_text:
Morocco beats Scotland in, scores fastest 2026 World Cup goal
Show Caption
Show Caption
Morocco has risen to the top of Group C after beating Scotland 1-0 in a pivotal World Cup group game on Friday.
Morocco led for almost the entire match after Ismael Saibari scored in the second minute. The ball he blasted by Angus Gunn was the game's lone goal, and was also the fastest scored to date at the 2026 World Cup.
Morocco controlled the ball and tempo early in the match, but Scotland began to generate momentum in the closing moments of the first half. The Tartan Army carried that into the second half, creating numerous chances to equalize the Atlas Lions, including a shot by Ryan Christie that just went over the top of the crossbar in the second half.
But ultimately, Yassine Bounou and Morocco kept a clean sheet. That gave the squad its first win at the 2026 World Cup and positioned itself to advance to the knockout stage with a positive result against Haiti.
Meanwhile, Scotland will enter its final group stage match against Brazil with three points to its name. Will that be enough to advance to the Round of 32, or will the Scots need to salvage at least a point to move on?
The Tartan Army will be hoping for a positive result either way as it looks to continue enjoying its first World Cup appearance since 1998.
Follow for live updates:
Saibari named Superior Player of the Match
No surprises here. Ismael Saibari, responsible for the lone goal here, was named the Superior Player of the Match.
FINAL: Morocco 1, Scotland 0
A tremendous three points for Morocco are in the bag after their 1-0 victory over Scotland. Ismael Saibari’s second-minute goal stood the test of time for the rest of the match to serve as the lone tally here.
Scotland finally strung together some chances in the final half hour of the match but never truly threatened. Morocco was the more physical team and the referee certainly let both sides play.
Six minutes of stoppage time remain
There will be a minimum of six minutes of stoppage time to end this game.
Morocco, Scotland make subs with time winding down
Morocco is still leading 1-0, and each team is making changes as it looks to improve its result. Below is a look at the subs made by each team.
Morocco:
Samir El Mourabet replaces Azzedine Ounahi
Scotland:
Ross Stewart replaces John McGinn
Anthony Ralston replaces Nathan Patterson
Scotland’s drive in final minutes
In the 85th minute, Scotland had a chance on goal with Scott McTominay’s shot being blocked and ricocheting into the side of the net. That led to a corner, which John McGinn placed into the box for a header attempt that also could not connect.
McTominay had another chance in the 88th minute when a bouncing ball lingered in the box, but the Moroccan defenders tied him up such that he could not muster full strength on his attempt that landed cleanly in the hands of keeper Yassine Bounou, who remained down for a moment after the play.
Goal-scorer Ismael Saibari subbed out along with two other Moroccan changes
Morocco subbed its lone goal-scorer, Saibari, out of the game in the 84th minute. Below are all three changes:
Soufiane Rahimi replaces Ismael Saibari
Ayoube Amaimouni replaces Brahim Diaz
Chemsdine Talbi replaces Bilal El Khannouss
Scotland appeals for penalty, doesn't get it
Scott McTominay felt as though he was fouled on a run toward net inside the 18-yard box. He went down, but the referee quickly signaled no foul had occurred.
McTominay was demonstrative behind the play about the lack of foul call while the Tartan Army booed the non-penalty decision.
Fans pay tribute to Donny Strathie in 76th minute
Longtime Tartan Army member Donny Strathie, from Grangemouth, Scotland, collapsed at a Boston hotel Sunday and died while in town for the World Cup. Strathie was 76 and was traveling with family and friends for the tournament, according to BBC.
Strathie was planning on attending Friday’s match against Morocco, which would have been his first time at a Scotland World Cup event.
Both Scotland and Morocco supporters on-hand for Friday's game gave Strathie a standing ovation when his photo and name were shown in the 76th minute.
Ben Gannon-Doak collides with Moroccan keeper chasing ball
Gannon-Doak was trying to get on the end of long-ball played up front, but Yassine Bounou beat him to the spot. Gannon-Doak couldn't slow up and ended up crashing hard into the Moroccan keeper.
Both players briefly remained down but were able to get up, no worse for wear. Play continued on, as 75 minutes have elapsed at Boston Stadium.
Scotland makes two more substitutions
Coming out of the hydration break, Scotland is making two more substitutions. They will be as follows:
Lyndon Dikes replaces Che Adams
Kenny McLean replaces Ryan Christie
Morocco leading Scotland 1-0 at hydration break
Ismael Saibari's second-minute goal remains the only scoring of the day in Morocco vs. Scotland. As a result, the Atlas Lions are leading 1-0, but Scotland has slowly built momentum and generated chances while keeper Angus Gunn continues to keep his side in the match.
Will Scotland be able to break through? That will determine whether it can remain atop Group C or if Morocco will rise to the top slot with its first 2026 World Cup win.
Andy Robertson gets yellow card
Scotland has had its first player booked of the day. Andy Robertson picked up a yellow card after tackling a Moroccan player as he made his way up the right side of the pitch.
Each team has now received one yellow card with 67 minutes gone.
Ryan Christie gets Scotland's best chance but sends it just high of net
Christie took a shot from just outside the center of the 18-yard box and did well to find a lane through traffic and create a high-danger shot. It went just barely over the crossbar, but the shot still represented one of Scotland's best chances of the day.
Scotland still trails 1-0 in the 65th minute.
Morocco shot goes wide after free kick
Morocco got a free kick in a dangerous area just to the left of Scotland's 18-yard box. They weren't able to get a chance inside the box, but eventually Achraf Hakimi found a late and attempted a shot. It went wide right of the net.
Scotland's Kieran Tierney receives medical attention before being subbed off
Tierney went down early in the second half and briefly received medical attention before getting up and walking off the pitch under his own power. Scotland opted to sub him off, bringing Ben Gannon-Doak into the match to replace him.
Moroccan player has jersey ripped during play
Scotland vs. Morocco has been a physical battle. Case in point, one of the players nearly had his jersey ripped clean off during play.
Ismael Saibari nearly scores again, Angus Gunn makes great save
Ismael Saibari nearly had his second goal of the match. About five minutes into the second half, Saibari took a cross and his shot deflected off a Scottish defender and off the top-left corner of the crossbar.
As Morocco continued its pressure and lined up for a corner kick, Scotland fans booed the pace in which it prepared for the set piece. Achraf Hakimi nearly beat Angus Gunn, who had to save the header.
Scotland vs. Morocco second half about to start
Halftime is just about wrapped up, as both Scotland and Morocco are returning to the field for the second half. Morocco is leading 1-0 after second-minute goal from Ismael Saibari, but Scotland built some momentum toward the end of the first half.
Why is Morocco MAR?
Those looking for a bit of halftime trivia may like to learn why Morocco's abbreviation for FIFA competition is "MAR."
The reason? Morocco is known as "Maroc" in French. Naturally, the first three letters of that country name are "MAR," which accounts for the team's FIFA abbreviation.
HALFTIME: Morocco 1, Scotland 0
Ismael Saibari’s second-minute goal is the lone tally in this one through the first 45 minutes (plus five minutes of stoppage time), and Morocco has threatened plenty against the Scottish, with five shots total.
But on two of those key chances, Morocco has skied shots over the net. On a different attack, in the 10th minute, a beautifully placed cross through the middle of the penalty area could not find the foot of a teammate.
If the result holds, Scotland and Morocco would be tied in the Group C standings with four points apiece, but Morocco would obviously hold the head-to-head tiebreaker.
Scotland does not have a shot and lacked any significant chance until stoppage time when John McGinn got his foot on a cross but couldn’t control it toward the net.
John McGinn fires chance wide of net but fires up Scottish crowd
Scotland is starting to generate some momentum, as it has now generated two chances in the first half's waning minutes. The most recent saw McGinn get on the end of a well-hit cross, though he was unable to get it on net.
The chance generated a hearty cheer from the Tartan Army, who have been largely subdued after falling behind early.
Scotland gets free-kick cross into 18-yard box, can't get it on net
The Scots got one of their best chances of the first half in the 44th minute. A Morocco foul set Scotland up for a free kick just outside of the 18-yard box. The Scots fed a quality cross in toward net, but nobody was able to fully get on the end of it.
Bilal El Khannouss fires shot over net
Morocco generated yet another high-danger chance but failed to get it on net, as it took a big bounce just as it got to El Khannouss. He still managed to get a solid strike on the ball but it sailed well over the crossbar.
Morocco sails shot over goal after brilliant run from Brahim Diaz
Diaz had a nice run toward net, weaving his way through the Scottish defense down the right side of the pitch. He eventually passed the ball and got it back in the 18-yard box before laying it off for Neil El Aynaoui.
El Aynaoui uncorked a shot, but it went well over the crossbar, keeping the score at 1-0 in favor of Morocco.
Rob Gronkowski in attendance for Scotland vs. Morocco
Legendary New England Patriots tight end Rob Gronkowski is in attendance for Friday's Scotland vs. Morocco game. He was shown on video during the first-half hydration break.
Morocco leading Scotland 1-0 at first-half hydration break
Ismael Saibari got Morocco on the board in the second minute and the Atlas Lions have controlled the game from there. They have largely dominated possession, though Scotland had a nice run with the ball in the lead-up to the hydration break.
Morocco has gotten a few high-danger chances but have thus far only turned one of its opportunities into a goal. It will be looking to change that as the first half continues while Scotland will be hoping to create some positive momentum with the ball in their possession.
Morocco's Issa Diop slapped with yellow card after hard tackle
Scotland had its first extended run of possession of Friday's match and turned it into a free kick opportunity between the midfield stripe and the 18-yard box.
Diop made a hard tackle that brought on the free kick, and was given a yellow card for the infraction.
Morocco dominating possession early vs. Scotland
Across the game's first 20 minutes, Morocco has had the ball a majority of the time, holding roughly 63% possession to Scotland's 28%, with the remaining 9% being contested.
As a result, Morocco has gotten the better scoring chances early in the contest and leads 1-0.
Angus Gunn makes save as Morocco again draws high-danger chance
Achraf Hakimi managed to get a shot on goal after getting behind the Scottish defense again. Gunn was coming out and trying to play the ball, but managed to adjust after losing the race and deflect away from net.
Morocco misses key chance
In the 10th minute, Morocco had another excellent chance to find the back of the net once again. Azzedine Ounahi ran onto a ball on the left side of the penalty area and laid a ball across the net, but two Moroccan attackers darting toward the goalpost could not put feet on the pass.
Ismael Saibari scores fastest goal of 2026 World Cup
Saibari's game-opening goal against Scotland is the fastest of the 2026 World Cup thus far. He scored in the second minute, and just about 72 seconds into the action.
That said, Saibari's goal isn't the fastest in World Cup history. Turkey's Hakan Şükür scored a goal just 11 seconds into his country's 3-2 win over South Korea at the 2002 World Cup.
Goal! Morocco opens scoring with second-minute goal
It didn't take Morocco long to get on the board. Just about 72 seconds into the game, Ismael Saibari got behind the Scottish defense and was able to fire a shot into the top left corner of the net.
Saibari has opened the scoring in each of Morocco's World Cup matches thus far, as he scored in the 21st minute against Brazil. The Atlas Lions will look to build upon that lead and protect it as they search for their first World Cup win of 2026.
Scottish fans give heart rendition of 'Flower of Scotland' as national anthems play
The Scottish fans belted out "Flower of Scotland" proudly in the moments ahead of the Scotland vs. Morocco match. They have the advantage in terms of support at Boston Stadium, but a strong Morocco contingent will oppose them during Friday's game.
Morocco and Scotland lineups being announced
Both teams are having their lineups announced for Friday's match. Each squad was met with cheers from the crowd, which appears to be trending toward a near, 50-50 split, with perhaps a slight Scottish edge.
Scotland plans to honor fan who died in Boston
Longtime Tartan Army member Donny Strathie, from Grangemouth, Scotland, collapsed at a Boston hotel Sunday and died while in town for the World Cup. Strathie was 76 and was traveling with family and friends for the tournament, according to BBC.
Strathie was planning on attending Friday’s match against Morocco, which would have been his first time at a Scotland World Cup event.
The family has asked Scottish to honor Strathie with applause during the 76th minute of the match. – Chris Bumbaca
Morocco takes field for warmups, draws cheers
The Tartan Army is out in full force to support Scotland, but Morocco's supporters also have a solid presence at Boston Stadium. As the Atlas Lions took the field, they received cheers of encouragement from the Morocco fans.
Scotland enters field to raucous cheers from crowd
Scotland has taken the field at Boston Stadium for its game against Morocco. The Tartan Army gave their squad a rowdy cheer and sang along to "Freed From Desire" as Scotland took the field.
Palpable energy at Boston Stadium for Scotland vs Morocco
The energy outside of Boston Stadium – dozens of miles outside of Boston and normally called Gillette Stadium – is palpable before this one. Scotland’s takeover of New England and beyond has been well-documented, but Morocco will be well-represented in the stands.
The typically jovial Scottish fans meshed well with the enthusiastic Moroccan supporters to create a friendly atmosphere. Elsewhere outside the stadium, fans watched as the United States defeated Australia 2-0 on a giant video board while a DJ blasted some beats. – Chris Bumbaca
Scotland lineup today

3 Andy Robertson
4 Scott McTominay
5 Grant Hanley

Scotland vs Morocco prediction, World Cup picks
Seth Vertelney: Scotland 1-2 Morocco
Morocco will be full of confidence after a very strong performance against Brazil. Scotland, meanwhile, held on for a win against Haiti. The Scots may struggle with the step up in opposition.
Jon Arnold: Scotland 0-2 Morocco
After a great opener, Scotland won't be able to deny a Morocco team trying to get more out of this game than it did from its opener.
Jesse Yomtov: Scotland 1-2 Morocco
Scotland's friendly takeover of Boston ends with a loss against a Morocco side with hopes of winning the group.
Victoria Hernandez: Scotland 1-2 Morocco
Ismael Saibari got Morocco's World Cup off to a quick start. The team will carry that momentum into this match.
Where is Scotland vs Morocco game?
The Group C match is being held at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts.
Scotland vs Morocco odds
World Cup standings
Explore live group standings, stats and the updated bracket with USA TODAY Sports here.
When does Scotland play today?
Scotland faces Morocco at 6 p.m. ET in the World Cup group stage on June 19.
When was last time Scotland qualified for World Cup?
Prior to qualifying for the 2026 tournament, Scotland's last World Cup appearance came at France 1998.
Achraf Hakimi to stand trial for rape
Paris Saint-Germain defender Achraf Hakimi said he faced trial on a rape charge and welcomed the opportunity to put across his point of view.
Hakimi, 27, who played for Morocco during its World Cup opener against Brazil on Sunday, had appealed his referral to criminal court. French media reported earlier that the Versailles appeals court had rejected the appeal, paving the way for his trial in a criminal court at some point in the future.
"Today, a story that isn't mine is being told at the expense of my family, my life, and above all, the truth. Sometimes I feel like I've become an easy target,"Hakimi wrote in a social media post. "I've been waiting for this trial since day one. And now I'm looking forward to it. Finally, I'll be able to speak out."
Where to watch Scotland vs Morocco
The game is airing on Fox, but you can also watch on Fubo or the Fox One App . Spanish language broadcasts are available on Telemundo and Peacock .
Watch every World Cup game with Fubo
What time is Scotland vs Morocco game?
The matchup kicks off at 6:00 PM ET.
_note: 网页赛报正文逐字留底（已去直播/电视/版权/导航杂讯）。
